title: Meeting People
description: Introduce yourself, ask names, and make a good first impression in Korean.
situation: First meetings: names, where you are from, age and seniority cues, and polite small talk.

phrases.tip: Literally 'I am meeting you for the first time'; very polite and standard.
Friendlier than 처음 뵙겠습니다, good with peers.
More polite: 성함이 어떻게 되세요? (seonghami eotteoke doeseyo?).
Use 이에요 after a consonant, 예요 after a vowel: 마크예요, 민준이에요.
Swap the country: 미국에서 왔어요 (the US), 영국에서 왔어요 (the UK).
Age sets the speech level and seniority, so this is a normal early question in Korea.

phrases.english: What do you do for work?
Do you have a business card?
phrases.tip: Receive a card with both hands and a small bow; it is a sign of respect.
Casual closing; with seniors prefer 또 뵙겠습니다 (tto boepgesseumnida).

phrases.english: I look forward to working with you
phrases.tip: A warm set phrase said after introductions; signals goodwill and cooperation.
